title: Syzygium sakalavarum
intro: Syzygium sakalavarum, also known as Sakalavary or Wild Syzygium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Syzygium sakalavarum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Syzygium sakalavarum is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or syrup, and is used to treat respiratory infections, digestive issues, and to reduce fever.
taxonomy: Syzygium sakalavarum is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to Madagascar and is also known as the Sakalava cherry. The classification of Syzygium sakalavarum places it within the genus Syzygium, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the 'water apple' or 'jambu'. The genus Syzygium is part of the larger family Myrtaceae, which includes over 150 genera and more than 3,000 species. Syzygium sakalavarum is a member of the order Myrtales, a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world.
